mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, consumer spending power deteriorates and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in >60% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and investment demand, which forces equity valuations lower over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day returns with an accuracy exceeding 55%, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: In a liquidity crisis regime, any stabilization or rebound in industrial production signals restored supply chain functionality and immediate market relief, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO rebounds fail to generate positive 5-day forward returns in >50% of observed instances, or the mechanism shows no predictive power during high-volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, which systematically drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS changes falls below 0.01, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >65% of months with positive payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 215.0, inflation expectations force central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances, increasing discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 215.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in multivariate regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent contraction in real GDP and real retail sales confirms a broad-based demand shock that depresses corporate earnings outlooks, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a hit rate above 58%, or the interaction term is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: During a financial crisis regime, rapid increases in the unemployment rate trigger automatic stabilizers and fiscal stimulus expectations, which support equity markets and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rising unemployment during crisis periods fails to predict positive 20-day returns, or the mechanism shows negative predictive accuracy in high-stress macro environments.
